terrain_lab_side_button Description: Click repeatedly: top GIS toolbar; toolbar plus general settings; off.
terrain_lab_module_project_description: Inspect the current WBXGEO project and import portable packages from the exchange folder.
terrain_lab_module_parameters_descriptio: Edit numeric brush, hydrology, bounded live-water, and integer erosion parameters.
terrain_lab_module_layers_description: Inspect core and derived layers together with their calculation readiness.
terrain_lab_module_settings_description: Inspect the format version, map limit, and WorldBox/QGIS exchange folders.
terrain_lab_toolbar_menu_description: Open the TerrainLab window for project details, parameters, the layer catalog, and settings.
terrain_lab_section_project_description: Show WBXGEO, GeoTIFF, and QGIS file synchronization commands.
terrain_lab_section_terrain_description: Show the inspector and signed-integer DEM editing tools.
terrain_lab_section_digitizing_descripti: Show the eyedropper, connected fill, and surface geometry tools.
terrain_lab_section_analysis_description: Show relief and hydrology calculations, bounded live-water routing, and erosion controls.
terrain_lab_section_layers_description: Show relief, drainage, and erosion-derived map layers.
terrain_lab_toolbar_relief_job_descripti: Calculate slope, aspect, ruggedness, and hillshade; click again to cancel the running job.
terrain_lab_toolbar_hydrology_job_descri: Calculate Priority-Flood, D8 flow, accumulation, streams, and basins; click again to cancel.
terrain_lab_toolbar_erosion_job_descript: Calculate integer erosion and deposition without changing the DEM; click again to cancel.
terrain_lab_toolbar_hide_overlays_descri: Remove the active relief, hydrology, or erosion overlay without deleting its calculated data.
terrain_lab_image_workspace_formats_form: Supported formats: {0}. SVG, PDF, PSD, and archives are not accepted.
terrain_lab_action_open_image_workspace_: Open TerrainLab's watched image folder in the operating-system file browser. This command only shows the folder: selecting a file there does not select it in the game. Use Choose image for classification and confirm the file inside TerrainLab. Accepted formats are PNG, JPG/JPEG/JFIF, TIFF/TIF, WebP, BMP, GIF, TGA, DDS, and JP2.
terrain_lab_action_toggle_image_watcher_: Repeat-click to enable or disable watched-folder import. Stable images are converted one at a time into new WorldBox save slots; the open world is not modified. The green lamp means watching and amber means converting.
terrain_lab_action_enable_image_watcher_: Watch the image folder and convert stable files one at a time with the safe terrain palette and an aspect-preserving size within the map memory budget. Every image becomes a new WorldBox save slot; the open world is not modified.
terrain_lab_action_disable_image_watcher: Stop scanning for new images after the current converter process finishes. Existing save slots and source images are kept.
terrain_lab_action_retry_images_descript: Clear remembered failures and queue each unchanged failed image again. Enable the watcher and verify that the current ImageToMap Python package is installed first.
terrain_lab_action_save_description: Open the save form, change the world name, and write the current DEM and module data to WBXGEO beside the ordinary WorldBox map.
terrain_lab_save_dialog_existing_slot: Change the world name and confirm overwriting the current slot. The ordinary WorldBox map and WBXGEO are saved together.
terrain_lab_save_dialog_new_slot: Enter the world name. After confirmation, WorldBox opens its standard new-slot picker.
terrain_lab_save_name_description: The name shown in WorldBox's world list and written to map metadata. Up to 80 characters are accepted; < and > are forbidden.
terrain_lab_save_dialog_confirm_descript: Apply the entered name and save the map. For a world without a slot, the standard WorldBox slot picker opens next.
terrain_lab_save_dialog_cancel_descripti: Close the form without changing the name or saving.
terrain_lab_status_save_dialog_opened: Enter the world name and confirm saving
terrain_lab_status_choose_save_slot: Name applied; choose a WorldBox slot
terrain_lab_action_export_description: Create a portable WBXGEO package in the exchange folder; embedded map.wbox remains a normal WorldBox map.
terrain_lab_action_validate_description: Check the current WBXGEO package schema, dimensions, cell budget, and integrity.
terrain_lab_action_open_exchange_descrip: Open the portable WBXGEO package exchange folder in the file browser.
terrain_lab_action_import_description: Create a compatible WorldBox save slot from the selected portable WBXGEO package.
terrain_lab_layer_elevation_description: Open the signed Int16 DEM layer, its properties, editor, and relief derivatives.
terrain_lab_layer_landform_description: Inspect the categorical surface-form layer, independent of absolute elevation.
terrain_lab_layer_material_description: Inspect the categorical soil, sand, rock, ice, and related material layer.
terrain_lab_layer_vanilla_description: Inspect the embedded map.wbox used by players without TerrainLab.
terrain_lab_layer_landform_semantics: Plain, hill, mountain, channel and related forms
terrain_lab_layer_material_semantics: Soil, sand, rock, ice and surface classes
terrain_lab_tool_inspect_description: Show coordinates, elevation, and the active derived value under the pointer; click again to deselect the tool.
terrain_lab_tool_set_description: Write the selected elevation into every brush cell; 9999 is unavailable because it is reserved for NODATA.
terrain_lab_tool_raise_description: Increase brush-cell elevations by the configured step without changing surface morphotypes.
terrain_lab_tool_lower_description: Decrease brush-cell elevations by the configured step without changing surface morphotypes.
terrain_lab_tool_smooth_description: Reduce local elevation discontinuities in the signed-integer DEM inside the brush.
terrain_lab_tool_ramp_description: Click a valid start cell, then click an endpoint to grade the DEM from the sampled start elevation to the configured target across the current brush width. Right-click or Escape cancels the anchor.
terrain_lab_elevation_value_description: Absolute target DEM elevation in metres. Higher values place the edited terrain higher. Allowed range: -20000 to 9000 m; 9999 is reserved for NODATA.
terrain_lab_elevation_step_description: Metres added or removed by one Raise or Lower operation. A larger value changes relief faster and creates sharper height differences. Allowed range: 1 to 32767 m.
terrain_lab_brush_radius_description: Distance in cells from the cursor affected by editing. Radius 0 changes one cell; a larger radius changes a wider area and costs more processing. Allowed range: 0 to 16 cells.
terrain_lab_action_radius_decrease_descr: Decrease the GIS brush radius by one cell.
terrain_lab_action_radius_increase_descr: Increase the GIS brush radius by one cell.
terrain_lab_action_undo_description: Undo the latest TerrainLab DEM or gameplay-safe surface edit.
terrain_lab_action_redo_description: Reapply the latest TerrainLab edit that was undone.
terrain_lab_elevation_overlay_descriptio: Show the translucent DEM on the fixed -20000..9000 m Turbo scale: negative elevations are blue and positive relief is yellow to red; one pixel represents one cell. Click again to hide.
terrain_lab_elevation_overlay_empty: The DEM has no displayable elevation values
terrain_lab_data_overlay_landform_descri: Color every valid cell by its TerrainLab morphotype: plains, uplands, mountains, channels, depressions, cliffs, and other forms. Click again to hide.
terrain_lab_data_overlay_material_descri: Color every valid cell by its gameplay-safe material class such as soil, sand, rock, ice, or organic cover. Click again to hide.
terrain_lab_data_overlay_contours_descri: Draw 250 m contours directly from the authoritative DEM; 1000 m contours are gold and sea level is cyan. Click again to hide.
terrain_lab_data_overlay_managed_water_d: Show only cells created and managed by TerrainLab live-water routing. The mask updates as channels grow or dry. Click again to hide.
terrain_lab_data_overlay_water_storage_d: Show the persistent UInt8 live-water reserve from shallow cyan through deep blue to maximum magenta. The layer updates with routing and evaporation. Click again to hide.
terrain_lab_data_overlay_hydro_feature_d: Show persistent freshwater semantics independently of absolute elevation: cyan channels and blue impounded waterbodies remain defined after drying. Click again to hide.
terrain_lab_data_overlay_moisture_descri: Show the dynamic UInt8 substrate-moisture field used for drying, retention, and soil degradation. Click again to hide.
terrain_lab_data_overlay_erodibility_des: Show nonlinear dynamic erodibility from resistant green through yellow to readily eroded red. Click again to hide.
terrain_lab_data_overlay_local_slope_des: Show compact Horn 3 x 3 local slope encoded from 0 to pi/2 radians for every valid DEM cell. Click again to hide.
terrain_lab_data_overlay_local_aspect_de: Show compact downslope direction encoded from 0 to 2*pi radians with a cyclic color wheel. Click again to hide.
terrain_lab_data_overlay_empty: The selected layer has no visible cells
terrain_lab_elevation_map_status_format: X {0}  Y {1}  Z {2}  DEM Turbo  min {3}  sea {4}  max {5}
terrain_lab_error_elevation_value: Elevation must be from -20000 to 9000 m; 9999 is reserved for NODATA.
terrain_lab_hydrology_not_computed: Drainage analysis has not been run
terrain_lab_hydrology_stale: DEM changed; hydrology must be recomputed
terrain_lab_hydrology_cancel_description: Stop the running Priority-Flood/D8 calculation without deleting the latest completed result.
terrain_lab_hydrology_threshold_descript: Minimum number of upstream cells required to classify a cell as a stream. A higher threshold shows fewer, larger rivers; a lower threshold produces a denser drainage network. Allowed range: 1 to the project cell count.
terrain_lab_hydrology_run_description: Calculate depression fill, D8 direction, accumulation, streams, basins, and Strahler order from the current DEM.
terrain_lab_hydrology_overlay_streams_de: Calculate hydrology when needed, then show cells whose flow accumulation reaches the stream threshold; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_hydrology_overlay_accumulati: Calculate hydrology when needed, then show how many upstream cells drain through each cell; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_hydrology_overlay_fill_descr: Calculate hydrology when needed, then show the depth added by Priority-Flood to remove local depressions; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_hydrology_overlay_filled_ele: Calculate hydrology when needed, then show the complete non-destructive Priority-Flood surface on the fixed DEM color scale; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_hydrology_overlay_flow_direc: Calculate hydrology when needed, then color each draining cell by one of the eight deterministic receiver directions; outlets remain transparent. Click again to hide.
terrain_lab_hydrology_overlay_hide_descr: Hide the active hydrology overlay without deleting the calculated data.
terrain_lab_hydrology_threshold_error: Threshold must be between 1 and the project cell count.
terrain_lab_hydrology_map_status_format: X {0}  Y {1}  Z {2}  A {3:N0}  fill {4}  D8 {5}  {6}  basin {7}  order {8}
terrain_lab_action_export_gis_descriptio: Export current project rasters to the GIS directory, including signed Int16 elevation.tif.
terrain_lab_action_prepare_sync_descript: Create the baseline and working elevation.tif for controlled editing in QGIS.
terrain_lab_action_pull_sync_description: Apply incoming elevation.tif only when the world DEM has not changed since the baseline was created.
terrain_lab_action_pull_branch_descripti: On conflict, preserve a branch of the current DEM and then apply incoming elevation.tif.
terrain_lab_action_open_sync_description: Open the TerrainLab/QGIS file synchronization workspace.
terrain_lab_status_sync_conflict: World DEM changed after the sync baseline
terrain_lab_relief_not_computed: Relief derivatives have not been calculated
terrain_lab_relief_stale: DEM changed; relief derivatives are stale
terrain_lab_relief_cancel_description: Stop the running derivative calculation without deleting the latest completed result.
terrain_lab_relief_run_description: Calculate hypsometry, slope, aspect, ruggedness, and hillshade from the current DEM with Horn 3 x 3 derivatives.
terrain_lab_relief_overlay_hypsometry_de: Calculate relief derivatives when needed, then color the map by absolute elevation; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_relief_overlay_slope_descrip: Calculate relief derivatives when needed, then show Horn surface steepness in degrees; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_relief_overlay_aspect_descri: Calculate relief derivatives when needed, then show the direction of maximum downslope descent; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_relief_overlay_hillshade_des: Calculate relief derivatives when needed, then show analytical DEM shading; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_relief_overlay_ruggedness_de: Calculate relief derivatives when needed, then show local maximum elevation difference from yellow through orange to dark red; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_relief_overlay_hide_descript: Hide the active relief overlay without deleting the calculated derivatives.
terrain_lab_relief_map_status_format: X {0}  Y {1}  Z {2}  slope {3:0.0} deg  aspect {4:0.0} deg  rugged {5} m
terrain_lab_hydrology_overlay_watersheds: Calculate hydrology when needed, then color cells by their common drainage basin; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_hydrology_overlay_stream_ord: Calculate hydrology when needed, then show Strahler order in the stream network; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_erosion_not_computed: Erosion preview has not been calculated
terrain_lab_erosion_stale: DEM changed; erosion preview is stale
terrain_lab_erosion_cancel_description: Stop the running preview calculation; the world DEM remains unchanged.
terrain_lab_erosion_iterations_descripti: Number of erosion and deposition passes. More iterations develop landforms further but increase calculation time. Allowed range: 1 to 100.
terrain_lab_erosion_flow_strength_descri: Strength of material transport along calculated drainage. A higher percentage cuts channels and deposits sediment more strongly; 0 disables this process. Allowed range: 0 to 100%.
terrain_lab_erosion_thermal_strength_des: Strength of gravity-driven movement from steep cells to lower neighbors. A higher percentage smooths steep slopes faster; 0 disables this process. Allowed range: 0 to 100%.
terrain_lab_erosion_talus_description: Minimum neighboring elevation difference before slope material starts moving. A higher value preserves steeper slopes; a lower value smooths more terrain. Allowed range: 0 to 32767 m.
terrain_lab_erosion_run_description: Calculate integer hydraulic and slope transport with exact mass-balance checks without changing the DEM.
terrain_lab_erosion_overlay_net_descript: Calculate hydrology and erosion when needed, then show total elevation change; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_erosion_overlay_cut_descript: Calculate hydrology and erosion when needed, then show cells that lose material; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_erosion_overlay_fill_descrip: Calculate hydrology and erosion when needed, then show cells that gain material; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_erosion_overlay_result_descr: Calculate hydrology and erosion when needed, then preview the resulting DEM; click again to hide.
terrain_lab_erosion_overlay_hide_descrip: Hide the erosion preview layer without deleting the calculated result.
terrain_lab_erosion_apply_description: Write the current erosion result into the DEM and mark dependent calculations as stale.
terrain_lab_erosion_mass_balance_error: Preview failed the exact mass-balance check
terrain_lab_erosion_percent_error: Strength must be from 0 to 100 percent.
terrain_lab_tool_surface_sample_descript: Store the safe surface under the pointer for fill and geometry tools; destructive gameplay tiles are rejected.
terrain_lab_tool_surface_fill_descriptio: Replace the entire four-connected region of one surface with the sampled surface without changing the DEM.
terrain_lab_tool_surface_line_descriptio: Left-click vertices; right-click or press Enter to paint the sampled surface, and Backspace to remove a vertex.
terrain_lab_tool_surface_polygon_descrip: Left-click polygon vertices; right-click or press Enter to fill it with the sampled safe surface.
terrain_lab_tool_surface_rectangle_descr: Left-click two opposite corners to fill a rectangle with the sampled safe surface.
terrain_lab_tool_polygonize_description: Select the complete four-connected surface region under the pointer for a later sample application.
terrain_lab_action_apply_selection_descr: Replace the polygonized selection with the gameplay-safe surface previously stored by the eyedropper.
terrain_lab_action_cancel_digitizing_des: Clear unfinished geometry and the polygonized selection without changing the map.
terrain_lab_status_ramp_started_format: Ramp anchor: {0} m; endpoint target: {1} m
terrain_lab_error_surface_sample_require: Sample a safe surface with the eyedropper first.
terrain_lab_error_ramp_nodata: A DEM ramp cannot start on a NODATA cell.
terrain_lab_toolbar_water_dynamics_descr: Toggle finite-volume river routing. Marine classes follow absolute DEM: shallow 0..-5 m, shelf -6..-149 m, deep ocean at -150 m and below. Freshwater gets persistent river or waterbody semantics at any elevation, incises channels, erodes one-to-two-cell alluvial banks, and forms a connected terminal lake when no outlet exists. The area limit is user-selectable from 1 to 100%. Destroyed geysers stop feeding their rivers, which then drain into sandy ravines.
terrain_lab_water_maximum_flood_percent_: Maximum share of valid DEM cells that live water may control. A higher value permits wider rivers and larger terminal lakes; a lower value contains them sooner. User range: 1 to 100%. Water volume and terrain resistance still limit actual spread.
terrain_lab_water_initial_source_volume_: Finite water units added when existing water first contacts routable terrain. A higher value lets that source travel farther or fill deeper depressions. Each transformed cell pays its own depth and resistance cost. Allowed range: 1 to 4096.
terrain_lab_water_geyser_pulse_volume_de: Water units added by each native geyser burst. A higher value grows a river faster and sustains it farther from the geyser. Allowed range: 1 to 1024.
terrain_lab_water_cells_per_tick_descrip: Maximum terrain cells changed during one simulation tick. A higher value makes rivers advance faster but uses more CPU per frame; it does not create extra water. Allowed range: 1 to 512.
terrain_lab_water_evaporation_per_climat: Water-storage units removed every 30 seconds. A higher value dries channels and wetlands faster; 0 disables evaporation. Allowed range: 0 to 16.
terrain_lab_water_bank_erosion_radius_de: Width around an established river that is moistened and degraded into sandy or fine clay/silt alluvium. 1 changes the immediate bank; 2 also changes the next cell ring and costs more processing. Allowed range: 1 to 2 cells.
terrain_lab_water_orphaned_channel_drain: Extra water-storage units removed every 30 seconds after the geyser feeding a connected channel is destroyed. A higher value exposes the dry sandy channel and ravine shoulders sooner. This still works when normal evaporation is 0. Allowed range: 1 to 64.
terrain_lab_water_dynamics_stopped: Live DEM water disabled; existing water remains on the map
terrain_lab_water_routing_d8_description: Choose one steepest neighboring cell at each step. Produces the narrowest, fastest, most stable rivers, but bends follow the eight grid directions. Every new channel fragment is a connected three-cell line or corner. A blocked end merges into existing water or starts a connected local lake.
terrain_lab_water_routing_dinf_descripti: Estimate a continuous downslope direction and keep at most two receivers. The connected three-cell fragment follows the stronger receiver first, giving smoother bends and moderate branching. A blocked end merges into existing water or starts a connected local lake.
terrain_lab_water_routing_mfd_descriptio: Keep every lower neighbor as a possible route, weighted by slope. Each individual three-cell fragment stays connected, but later fragments can branch into a broader wet network. A blocked end merges into existing water or starts a connected local lake. This option uses the most processing.
terrain_lab_action_automatic_clustering_: Open the independent automatic interpretation workflow. Choose a workspace raster, optionally outline the useful map area, tune basic or expert clustering controls, and build a WorldBox map without manual training samples. Pixels outside the boundary are excluded as noise and become deep ocean.
terrain_lab_cluster_image_choice_descrip: Use the arrows to choose a supported raster already present in ImageWorkspace. The candidate is not loaded until Open selected is pressed. Each source keeps its own clustering profile and boundary.
terrain_lab_cluster_previous_image_descr: Highlight the previous workspace image without opening it.
terrain_lab_cluster_next_image_descripti: Highlight the next workspace image without opening it.
terrain_lab_cluster_open_selected_descri: Load the highlighted raster and its per-image clustering profile. This unlocks the boundary and parameter controls.
terrain_lab_cluster_fit_description: Reset zoom and pan so the complete source raster fits in the available workspace.
terrain_lab_cluster_canvas_description: Inspect the source and optionally digitize one area-of-interest boundary. Use the wheel to zoom, middle- or right-drag to pan, left-click to add vertices, and right-click or double-click to close the boundary.
terrain_lab_cluster_algorithm_legacy_des: Use the stable 1.x adaptive colour classifier. It analyzes the already resized WorldBox grid and preserves the exact behavior of profiles created before schema 4.
terrain_lab_cluster_algorithm_semantic_d: Analyze the source raster before map downsampling, keep terrain classes independent in diagnostics, enforce biotope hostability, and reduce categorical labels by area voting.
terrain_lab_cluster_boundary_draw_descri: Start a new area-of-interest polygon. Only pixels inside it are sampled and clustered; the noisy exterior becomes deep ocean. A completed new boundary replaces the previous one.
terrain_lab_cluster_boundary_finish_desc: Close and validate the current boundary. At least three distinct, non-self-intersecting vertices are required.
terrain_lab_cluster_boundary_cancel_desc: Discard only the unfinished boundary. The previously saved boundary and all clustering settings remain unchanged.
terrain_lab_cluster_boundary_clear_descr: Remove the saved area-of-interest boundary so the complete raster participates in clustering again.
terrain_lab_cluster_composition_toggle_d: Open a safe allow-list of morphotypes and WorldBox biotopes. Automatic clustering may use only checked classes; at least one morphotype and one biotope must remain enabled.
terrain_lab_cluster_composition_surfaces: Open the morphotype icon palette. A lit green marker means that automatic clustering may assign this surface class; press the icon again to exclude it.
terrain_lab_cluster_composition_biotopes: Open the WorldBox biotope icon palette. A lit green marker means that automatic clustering may plant this safe living biome; press the icon again to exclude it.
terrain_lab_cluster_budget_description: The first number is the effective maximum, the second is the selected total budget, and the pool is the number of unique classes enabled by the morphotype and biotope palettes. Water, shores, and land share one budget. When the pool is larger, source clusters are ranked by covered pixels: dominant colours and terrain groups keep their closest classes, while rare groups merge into the nearest dominant.
terrain_lab_cluster_expert_toggle_descri: Show or hide ten advanced feature weights, quality limits, and reproducibility controls. Their saved values remain active while the section is collapsed.
terrain_lab_cluster_clusters_description: Hard total limit for unique output classes, including deep water, shelf, shallow water, and land. Higher values preserve more map classes and small details, but increase fragmentation and processing time. If more palette classes are enabled, dominant source groups are kept and rare groups merge into them. Recommended range: 8 to 20.
terrain_lab_cluster_spline_radius_descri: Radius of the multiscale smoothing neighbourhood in output map cells. Higher values suppress labels, scan grain, and colour speckles, but can erase narrow rivers and small terrain patches. Zero disables spline smoothing.
terrain_lab_cluster_smooth_passes_descri: Number of majority-filter passes applied after clustering. Higher values remove isolated output cells and round boundaries, but progressively simplify narrow objects.
terrain_lab_cluster_min_land_region_desc: Connected land regions smaller than this output-cell count are treated as noise and converted to water. Increase it to remove tiny islands; use zero to preserve every land fragment.
terrain_lab_cluster_water_sensitivity_de: Scales how readily dark, blue, and low-saturation clusters become water. Above 100 percent creates more water; below 100 percent protects ambiguous land. The map boundary remains authoritative.
terrain_lab_cluster_analysis_max_dimensi: Maximum source-raster side analyzed by Semantic v2, from 512 to 4096 pixels. Sources below the limit remain at native resolution unless the 12-megapixel safety cap applies. Higher values preserve finer linear features but increase memory use and processing time. Legacy v1 ignores this value.
terrain_lab_cluster_color_weight_descrip: Importance of full RGB colour distance. Increase it when map classes use distinct hues; reduce it for faded scans where paper tint and printing variation dominate.
terrain_lab_cluster_luma_weight_descript: Importance of light versus dark differences. Increase it for shaded-relief or elevation-tinted maps; reduce it when shadows and scanned paper folds should not define classes.
terrain_lab_cluster_saturation_weight_de: Importance of vivid versus muted colours. Increase it when saturated thematic fills carry meaning; reduce it for old or unevenly faded maps.
terrain_lab_cluster_texture_weight_descr: Importance of local colour roughness. Increase it to separate forests, hatching, or stippled terrain from smooth fills. Excessive values can classify labels and print grain as terrain.
terrain_lab_cluster_slope_weight_descrip: Importance of local image gradients and boundaries. Increase it to preserve relief edges and narrow linear objects; reduce it when contour lines and text create false classes.
terrain_lab_cluster_spatial_weight_descr: Preference for nearby pixels to share a cluster. Higher values create more compact regions and suppress scattered noise, but may split the same colour into different clusters solely because it occurs far apart.
terrain_lab_cluster_detail_weight_descri: Blend between the original raster and the spline-smoothed raster. At 100 percent all fine detail is retained; lower values follow broad colour fields and ignore more high-frequency noise.
terrain_lab_cluster_sample_limit_descrip: Maximum number of source pixels used to fit cluster centres. Higher values improve stability on large, complex rasters but use more memory and processing time; all output pixels are still classified.
terrain_lab_cluster_iterations_descripti: Maximum centre-refinement passes. More iterations can improve convergence on difficult colour scales but take longer and usually have diminishing benefit above 30.
terrain_lab_cluster_seed_description: Deterministic initial partition used by clustering. Keeping the same seed reproduces the same result; changing it explores another valid partition when clusters converge poorly.
terrain_lab_cluster_save_profile_descrip: Validate and write the selected engine, all 16 settings, composition, and optional area-of-interest boundary to a JSON sidecar associated only with this source image.
terrain_lab_cluster_build_map_descriptio: Save the profile and queue this raster for automatic conversion. Clustering is fully independent of manual annotations; pixels outside the optional boundary become deep ocean.
terrain_lab_cluster_close_description: Close the automatic clustering workspace. Saved settings and the completed boundary remain in the image-specific profile; an unfinished boundary is discarded.
terrain_lab_cluster_no_images: Add a supported image to the TerrainLab image workspace first.
terrain_lab_cluster_select_and_confirm: Choose a source, then press Open selected.
terrain_lab_cluster_summary_format: {4}; clusters: {0}; spline: {1} cells; boundary vertices: {2}; unfinished: {3}
terrain_lab_cluster_ready: Tune the controls, optionally outline the useful map area, then build the map.
terrain_lab_cluster_finish_boundary_firs: Finish or cancel the current boundary before this action.
terrain_lab_cluster_boundary_vertex_limi: The map boundary is limited to 256 vertices.
terrain_lab_cluster_boundary_drawing: Boundary digitizing started. Left-click vertices; right-click or double-click to finish.
terrain_lab_cluster_boundary_need_vertic: A map boundary needs at least three distinct vertices.
terrain_lab_cluster_boundary_missing: There is no saved map boundary to remove.
terrain_lab_cluster_boundary_cleared: Map boundary removed. The complete raster will be clustered.
terrain_lab_cluster_parameter_error: Enter a valid clustering value within the range described in its tooltip.
terrain_lab_cluster_invalid_fields_forma: Invalid model inputs: {0}. Correct the red fields.
terrain_lab_cluster_queued: Automatic clustering queued for the selected image.
terrain_lab_action_manual_classification: Open TerrainLab's in-game image selector, choose a workspace PNG, JPG/JPEG/JFIF, TIFF/TIF, WebP, BMP, GIF, TGA, DDS, or JP2, and press Open selected. The confirmed raster is shown over the world for point or QGIS-style polygon classification with an independent morphotype, biotope, and Int16 elevation.
terrain_lab_manual_no_images: Add a supported image to the TerrainLab image workspace first.
terrain_lab_manual_image_choice_descript: Use the left and right arrows to choose a supported file already present in ImageWorkspace. The centre field shows the current candidate once; the raster is not loaded and editing remains locked until Open selected is pressed.
terrain_lab_manual_open_selected_descrip: Confirm the highlighted file and load it as the translucent classification raster. Surface, biotope, elevation, annotation, and Build map controls unlock only after loading succeeds.
terrain_lab_manual_canvas_description: Choose Point, Line, or Polygon, digitize the geometry, then choose its attributes and publish it. A published object remains visible; the next object's geometry and attributes start unselected. Boundary mode outlines the map area of interest, and the separate outside-extent controls assign its exterior without training on it. Use the wheel to zoom and middle- or right-drag to pan.
terrain_lab_manual_previous_image_descri: Highlight the previous workspace image. This does not load it; press Open selected to confirm.
terrain_lab_manual_next_image_descriptio: Highlight the next workspace image. This does not load it; press Open selected to confirm.
terrain_lab_manual_fit_description: Reset zoom and pan so the complete source image fits in the available map workspace.
terrain_lab_manual_mode_point_descriptio: Place one precise source-pixel anchor for exceptional colours, small features, or a local DEM control point. The click creates an unpublished draft; choose all attributes and press Publish object to save it.
terrain_lab_manual_mode_polygon_descript: Digitize a training area as in GIS software. Left-click vertices, then right-click, double-click, or press Finish geometry. The closed draft changes its morphotype texture and DEM vertex colours live while attributes are selected. It reaches the profile only after Publish object.
terrain_lab_manual_mode_boundary_descrip: Outline the single map area of interest. Only pixels inside participate in adaptive clustering and manual training. Drawing a new boundary replaces the previous one; use the separate outside-extent controls to choose the morphotype, biotope, and elevation assigned outside it.
terrain_lab_manual_mode_delete_polygon_d: Activate polygon deletion, then left-click inside a saved training polygon to remove the topmost polygon at that location. The tool never removes point samples or the map boundary.
terrain_lab_manual_delete_all_polygons_d: Immediately remove every saved training polygon and any unfinished training polygon without a confirmation step. Point samples and the map boundary remain unchanged.
terrain_lab_manual_mode_delete_polygon_a: Polygon deletion mode active. Left-click a training polygon to remove it.
terrain_lab_manual_surface_description: The physical terrain class assigned to the next point or completed polygon. It is independent of colour and biotope, so visually similar areas can become different terrain when separate annotations are placed.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_description: Optional WorldBox biotope for soil surfaces. Auto retains the adaptive classifier result; None uses bare soil. Water, sand, hills, and rock morphotypes ignore incompatible biotopes.
terrain_lab_manual_elevation_description: Signed Int16 elevation attached to the next point or full completed polygon and used by DEM interpolation. Allowed range: -20000 to 9000 metres. Value 9999 is reserved for NODATA.
terrain_lab_manual_global_dem_descriptio: Enabled: point and polygon heights form a continuous IDW surface over the complete output grid. Disabled: only manually classified areas receive values. Add controls around major highs, lows, and shorelines; a polygon holds its selected elevation throughout its interior.
terrain_lab_manual_finish_polygon_descri: Close the current ring and assign its full area the selected morphotype, biotope, and elevation. At least three non-self-intersecting vertices are required. Right-click or double-click on the raster performs the same action.
terrain_lab_manual_cancel_polygon_descri: Discard only the unfinished polygon vertices. Previously saved points, polygons, and the source image remain unchanged.
terrain_lab_manual_finish_boundary_descr: Close and save the area-of-interest ring. At least three non-self-intersecting vertices are required. The new boundary replaces the saved one; pixels outside never train the classifier and receive the class selected in Outside map extent.
terrain_lab_manual_cancel_boundary_descr: Discard only the unfinished boundary vertices. The previously saved map boundary, training annotations, and source image remain unchanged.
terrain_lab_manual_remove_boundary_descr: Remove the saved area-of-interest boundary. The complete source image will again participate in clustering and classification.
terrain_lab_manual_undo_description: Remove the latest unfinished vertex first; otherwise remove the latest polygon, then the latest point. Completed profile changes are saved immediately.
terrain_lab_manual_clear_description: Remove every point, line, training polygon, and the map boundary. Press twice within three seconds to confirm; the source image is not deleted.
terrain_lab_manual_save_profile_descript: Write the validated JSON sidecar next to the source image. Coordinates, surface classes, biotopes, elevations, interpolation settings, and the optional map boundary are portable with the image.
terrain_lab_manual_build_map_description: Save the profile and force this image into the ImageToMap queue even if it was converted before. Outside-boundary pixels are excluded from clustering and training, then written with the separately selected outside morphotype, biotope, and elevation. The WorldBox save also receives an interpolated signed Int16 DEM GeoTIFF.
terrain_lab_manual_close_description: Hide the source-image overlay and its classification controls. Saved points, training polygons, and the map boundary remain in the workspace profile; an unfinished ring is discarded.
terrain_lab_manual_samples_format: Points: {0}/{1}; lines: {2}/{3}; polygons: {4}/{5}; draft vertices: {6}; boundary vertices: {7}
terrain_lab_manual_select_and_confirm: The current file is not loaded. Press Open selected.
terrain_lab_manual_ready: Choose a drawing or deletion mode and work directly on the source raster.
terrain_lab_manual_no_near_annotation: No point marker or polygon is close enough to remove.
terrain_lab_manual_polygon_need_vertices: A polygon needs at least three distinct vertices.
terrain_lab_manual_polygon_vertex_limit: A polygon is limited to 256 vertices.
terrain_lab_manual_no_polygon_at_point: There is no training polygon under this point.
terrain_lab_manual_polygon_removed_at_po: Training polygon removed. Point samples and the map boundary were preserved.
terrain_lab_manual_no_polygons: There are no training polygons to remove.
terrain_lab_manual_polygons_cleared_form: Removed {0} training polygon object(s). Point samples and the map boundary were preserved.
terrain_lab_manual_boundary_need_vertice: A map boundary needs at least three distinct vertices.
terrain_lab_manual_boundary_vertex_limit: The map boundary is limited to 256 vertices.
terrain_lab_manual_boundary_saved: Map boundary saved. Outside pixels will be ignored during learning and converted to deep ocean.
terrain_lab_manual_boundary_removed: Map boundary removed. The complete source image is active again.
terrain_lab_manual_outside_boundary: This point is outside the map boundary and cannot train the classifier.
terrain_lab_manual_finish_draft: Finish or cancel the open polygon or map boundary before building the map.
terrain_lab_manual_need_samples: Add at least two points, one line, or one polygon before building a map.
terrain_lab_manual_queued: Image queued with manual classification and DEM.
terrain_lab_manual_nothing_to_undo: There are no classification annotations to remove.
terrain_lab_manual_clear_confirm: Press Clear annotations again within three seconds.
terrain_lab_manual_cleared: All classification points, lines, polygons, and the map boundary removed.
terrain_lab_manual_elevation_error: Elevation must be an integer from -20000 to 9000 metres; 9999 is reserved for NODATA.
terrain_lab_manual_surface_deep_ocean_de: Ocean floor deeper than 150 m. This morphotype cannot receive a terrestrial biotope.
terrain_lab_manual_surface_shelf_descrip: Submerged continental shelf from -150 to -6 m. Terrestrial biome buttons are disabled.
terrain_lab_manual_surface_shallow_water: Marine shallows from -5 to -1 m. Terrestrial biome buttons are disabled.
terrain_lab_manual_surface_river_lake_de: Inland water independent of absolute elevation. Terrestrial biome buttons are disabled.
terrain_lab_manual_surface_sand_descript: Bare sand or beach terrain. Seeds are not assigned to this morphotype.
terrain_lab_manual_surface_plain_descrip: Low-relief living soil. Select one surface-biome icon for vegetation and seed behaviour.
terrain_lab_manual_surface_lowland_descr: Low-elevation living soil. Select one surface-biome icon for vegetation and seed behaviour.
terrain_lab_manual_surface_upland_descri: Elevated living soil. Select one surface-biome icon for vegetation and seed behaviour.
terrain_lab_manual_surface_hills_descrip: Hilly non-soil relief; terrestrial biome buttons are disabled.
terrain_lab_manual_surface_rocks_descrip: Mountain relief centred near 4500 m; terrestrial biome buttons are disabled.
terrain_lab_manual_surface_summit_descri: Extreme mountain terrain at 7000 m or above, limited to roughly the upper five percent of mountain cells.
terrain_lab_manual_surface_depression_de: A local basin represented by living low soil. Absolute elevation may still be above sea level.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_grass_descrip: Use the temperate grass surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_jungle_descri: Use the jungle surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_savanna_descr: Use the savanna surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_desert_descri: Use the desert surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_permafrost_de: Use the permafrost surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_swamp_descrip: Use the swamp surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_enchanted_des: Use the enchanted surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_lemon_descrip: Use the lemon surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_crystal_descr: Use the crystal surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_corrupted_des: Use the normal WorldBox corrupted seed biome; no destructive terrain tile is introduced.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_infernal_desc: Use the normal WorldBox infernal seed biome; no lava or destructive tile is introduced.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_candy_descrip: Use the candy surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_mushroom_desc: Use the mushroom surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_wasteland_des: Use the normal WorldBox wasteland seed biome without explosive or spreading hazard tiles.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_birch_descrip: Use the birch surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_maple_descrip: Use the maple surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_rocklands_des: Use the rocklands surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_garlic_descri: Use the garlic surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_flower_descri: Use the flower surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_celestial_des: Use the celestial surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_clover_descri: Use the clover surface biome and its normal WorldBox vegetation.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_singularity_d: Use the normal WorldBox singularity seed biome without adding destructive terrain tools.
terrain_lab_manual_biotope_paradox_descr: Use the normal WorldBox paradox seed biome without adding destructive terrain tools.
terrain_lab_manual_mode_line_description: Digitize a narrow or elongated training feature. Left-click two or more vertices, then right-click, double-click, or press Finish geometry. Width is stored in output WorldBox cells.
terrain_lab_manual_mode_line_active: Line digitizing is active. Add at least two vertices, then finish the geometry.
terrain_lab_manual_finish_geometry_descr: Finish the current line or close the current polygon without saving it yet. Then choose morphotype, biotope, elevation, and line width when applicable, and publish the object.
terrain_lab_manual_cancel_draft_descript: Discard only the current unpublished geometry and reset its selected attributes. Previously published objects remain visible and saved.
terrain_lab_manual_publish_feature_descr: Commit the visible point, line, or polygon with the selected attributes to this image's classification profile. After publication the object remains visible, while geometry and all object attributes reset to an unselected state.
terrain_lab_manual_line_width_descriptio: Authoritative width of the published line in output WorldBox cells. Larger values classify a wider corridor. Allowed range: 1 to 32.
terrain_lab_manual_outside_surface_descr: Surface assigned outside the published map polygon but inside its rectangular output extent. Deep ocean is the default. Auto continues the nearest interior class like a Voronoi field. Outside cells never train the classifier.
terrain_lab_manual_outside_auto_descript: Continue the nearest classified interior morphotype and biotope beyond the polygon. Elevation remains a smooth continuation of the interpolated DEM instead of using one fixed outside value.
terrain_lab_manual_outside_biotope_descr: Optional WorldBox biotope applied outside the map boundary when compatible with the selected morphotype. Use None for bare terrain or Auto to preserve adaptive biome selection where possible.
terrain_lab_manual_outside_elevation_des: Signed Int16 DEM elevation assigned outside the map boundary. Allowed range: -20000 to 9000 metres; 9999 remains reserved for NODATA.
terrain_lab_manual_outside_saved: Outside-map morphotype, biotope, and elevation saved.
terrain_lab_manual_choose_geometry: Choose Point, Line, or Polygon before digitizing an object.
terrain_lab_manual_publish_or_cancel_dra: Publish or cancel the current object before starting another geometry.
terrain_lab_manual_point_drafted_format: Point draft placed at X {0}, Y {1}. Choose all attributes, then publish it.
terrain_lab_manual_line_need_vertices: A line requires at least two distinct vertices.
terrain_lab_manual_line_vertex_limit: One line is limited to 256 vertices.
terrain_lab_manual_geometry_completed: Geometry completed but not saved. Choose every object attribute and press Publish object.
terrain_lab_manual_complete_geometry_fir: Digitize and finish a point, line, or polygon before publishing.
terrain_lab_manual_feature_published_for: {0} published. The saved object remains visible; geometry and object attributes were reset.
terrain_lab_manual_draft_cancelled: Unpublished geometry discarded; object attributes reset.
terrain_lab_manual_select_all_attributes: Select a morphotype and biotope and enter an elevation before publishing.
terrain_lab_manual_line_width_error: Line width must be an integer from 1 to 32 output cells.
terrain_lab_output_long_side_description: Sets the exact block count along the longer side of the generated WorldBox map. One block is 64 x 64 cells. The shorter side is calculated from the source raster proportions. Larger values preserve more detail but consume more memory.
terrain_lab_output_size_error: The requested long side is invalid or exceeds the displayed map-cell limit.
